commit_scope: Closes the install-as-named-callable-leaf NAMED half of the linux-as-recipes loadable-modules row. The protocol lands as pure recipe tissue (install-leaf.fk): an install offer carries (name, artifact, expected-interface) where the interface is a CHILD of the artifact — axiom-3 makes lying about an interface structural; the kernel acknowledges by binding the name in a callable table (the node ack is the artifact's body NodeID, the unforgeable capability handle), and a name collision (first-bind-wins) or interface mismatch acknowledges 0 with NO residue; an unbound name acknowledges nothing. The native carrier lands on the Go kernel (the lane that already dlopens): jit_install compiles the named closure through the existing jit.go .so lane (content-addressed plugin cache reused) and binds the artifact as a NativeEntry in k.natives AT RUNTIME — the kernel's own callable surface grows by offer, never by recompile; a subsequent call BY NAME (a name no closure serves) dispatches into generated code with the same ABI guards as the closure jit path. installed_leaf? lets Form code distinguish a leaf the surface grew from a build-time native. Linux's insmod composed from the five axioms, never ported — boot.fk's self-extension (an offer mints a larger image) carried to the callable surface (an offer grows the table).

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/channel.fk form-stdlib/install-leaf.fk form-stdlib/tests/install-leaf-band.fk
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/channel.fk form-stdlib/persistence.fk form-stdlib/cell-registry.fk form-stdlib/boot.fk form-stdlib/tests/boot-band.fk
local_validation.summary: install-leaf-band verdict 9 (9 claims), 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go = Rust = TypeScript: a fresh install acks the artifact NodeID; a call BY NAME realizes the artifact's value (parity vs walking the body directly); a collision under a bound name refuses with first-bind-wins and no residue; an offer through an interface the artifact does not carry refuses; an unbound name acknowledges nothing; the SAME artifact binds under a second name (names are query keys, one identity); 4 offers grew the table by exactly the 2 accepted; the rebuilt offer is node_eq to the original. Go witness verdict 10: jit_install compiled (defn il-probe (a b) (add (mul a b) 7)) to a .so and bound it as 'il-probe-native' in k.natives at runtime; (il-probe-native 6 7) = 49 = the recipe walk; the float lane agrees ((il-probe-native 2.5 4.0)*10 rounds equal both paths); rebind refused, build-time-native collision refused, arity-3 offer refused with nothing landing, absent closure acked nothing, a 3-arg call to the 2-arity leaf acked nothing. primitive-registry-band 63 with pins retuned 199 total / 162 lane-1 / 37 lane-0; boot-band 9 regression clean; go test ./... ok (285s).

observed_delta.bands: install-leaf-band 9 — 0 divergent; primitive-registry-band 63 — 0 divergent (pins 197->199, lane-0 35->37); boot-band 9 — 0 divergent (regression)
observed_delta.witness: form/scripts/jit-install-leaf-test.sh -> verdict 10 PASS (live .so install, call-by-name, int+float parity, three refusal shapes + interface-breach silence)
observed_delta.blueprints_registered: INSTALL-IFACE/INSTALL-ARTIFACT/INSTALL-OFFER/INSTALLED-LEAF at 1.2.99.1754-1757; bp tables regenerated for all three kernels
observed_delta.kernel_surface: form-kernel-go gains jit_install (env-aware, witness category) and installed_leaf? (compare); both registry lane-0 sibling-gap; k.installedLeaves tracks runtime-grown leaves; observe events observe/go/jit/install, install-refused, installed-dispatch, installed-guard-miss
observed_delta.ledger_row: linux-as-recipes (loadable-modules) install-as-named-callable-leaf — coordinator flips NAMED to RUNS after sibling merges

known_limitations.limitation: The native carrier runs on Go only — the kernel that already carries the dlopen lane. Rust and TS native carriers are open stones named in install-leaf.form (dlopen over emitted cdylib; wasm/worker module); the PROTOCOL is proven on all three kernels by the band.
known_limitations.follow_up: Build a sibling carrier when an ask needs it; the offer->bind->call-by-name shape does not change.

known_limitations.limitation: Installed leaves live for the kernel process lifetime (the .so is process-mapped). A durable install across boots is the protocol's table-store shape (persistence.fk idiom) + re-offer at boot — boot.fk's own lane, not yet composed end-to-end.
known_limitations.follow_up: Compose boot.fk + install-leaf.fk (a minted image whose cells are install offers replayed at boot) when a consumer needs persistent surface growth.

known_limitations.limitation: Unbinding is named (a new table minted without the leaf — never in-place mutation) but not carried on the Go lane; register_jit/unregister_jit cover the alias case only.
known_limitations.follow_up: An uninstall offer if a real flow wants one; first-bind-wins keeps the table honest meanwhile.

ci_validation.summary: PR gates run the three-way bands; sibling kernels keep the verdict honest.

deploy_validation.summary: form-stdlib + form-kernel-go ride the api rebuild; the lattice auto-ingests install-leaf.form on merge.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No route changes. The stdlib gains the install protocol; the Go kernel's callable surface can grow at runtime by offer; install-as-named-callable-leaf moves from NAMED to runnable recipe + band + live witness.
test_flows: install-leaf-band three-way: bind ack = artifact NodeID, call-by-name parity, collision refusal, first-bind-wins residue check, interface mismatch refusal, honest absence, one-artifact-two-names, growth-by-offer count, content-addressed offer identity.
jit-install-leaf-test.sh live: jit -> install -> call-by-name -> int+float parity -> rebind/native-collision/arity refusals -> absent-cell nothing -> interface-breach silence.

phase_gate.gate: the install protocol proven three-way as a band AND the artifact-install witnessed live on the Go carrier; ledger flip stays with the coordinator
phase_gate.next: coordinator retunes the loadable-modules row after all six sibling PRs merge; sibling carriers + boot-composed durable installs when asked
